candidates.name: N0_status_quo
candidates.description: keep Scenario E rows 1-18 in all three families; asymmetry justified by the measured street/terrain stops
candidates.verdict_reason: every seat is Scenario-E-validated; the east deficit is street-and-terrain-bounded (see asymmetry_justification); no validated seat is sacrificed for visual symmetry

candidates.name: N1_east_contour_extension
candidates.description: widen the east azimuth cap (az 85 → terrain/street stop) and re-march the east bays on their own contours
candidates.verdict_reason: contour walk finds ~342 ft of on-contour east arc before the 45° splay gate (a generous cap — the civic_bowl composition gate was 28°, so treat this as an upper bound); improves balance ratio to 0.96; requires re-running design_extended_bays with E_CAP widened, then Scenario E re-validation (Rules 3/5: not seats until emitted + validated)

candidates.name: N2_add_row19_all
candidates.description: extend all three families to row 19 (per-seat C10 93 mm, 95% pass — formal under the civic_core per-seat band)
candidates.row_continuity: intact; adds one row above the cross-aisle
candidates.verdict_reason: row 19 is per-seat formal (design_civic_core/perseat_bands.csv: C10 93 mm, 95% pass) but centreline-soft (86 mm); does not change the balance ratio; requires Scenario E re-validation

candidates.name: N3_trim_south_to_balance
candidates.description: drop south rows 17-18 to close the arc-length gap
candidates.verdict_reason: removes ~88 validated Band-A seats purely for plan symmetry; no terrain, street, sightline, or cost driver supports it — symmetry is not a site constraint here
